story_title: Iran nuclear programme and US-Iran negotiations
tldr: Headlines divide along three axes: most buckets foreground Trump's 'nuclear above economic pain' maximalism (POLITICAL); opposition and US outlets advertise physical strike damage and proliferation risk (SECURITY_DEFENSE); and opinion and religious outlets ask whether JCPOA or a ceasefire would have been better (EXTERNAL_REGULATION).

frames.sub_frame: Trump maximalism: nuclear programme above economic pain

evidence.quote: nuclear program more important than Americans' economic pain
stopping Iran's nuclear programme outweighs economic pain
Chinese, Iranian diplomats meet before US-Iran nuclear talk
'I don't think about Americans' financial situation'

frames.sub_frame: strike damage and nuclear site hardening

evidence.quote: strikes hit Iran sites tied to nuclear weapon work
new fortifications at tunnel complex near Natanz
any Iran deal must close plutonium pathway to nuclear bombs
Would We Be Better Off Today With the JCPOA?
future Iran-US deal to be radically different from JCPOA
Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal but Trump rejects it

bottom_line: The headline layer makes Trump's explicit trade-off ('nuclear above economic pain') the dominant advertised frame; opposition and US outlets foreground physical site damage and proliferation risk; while opinion and religious outlets uniquely ask whether the JCPOA — or a ceasefire — would have been better.
